item.title: Saturday WHOLESOME dump to help you with your mental health #419
item.description:
1. Such a nice guy
2
3. 10 million years evolutionary history of friendship between men summarized in 26 seconds (you know they were doin this shit in 300 000 B.C.)
4. ONE OF US
5. Awesome random interaction
6. We don't deserve these boys
8. Nom, nom nom
9. You are getting taken care of! Whether you like it or not :D
10. Brave girl
11. I am elephant
13. Meet the 26-year-old African spurred tortoise, Bon-Chan and his kind owner Mitani-san in Tsukishima.
14. I haven't implemented a puppy tax in a log time. Sorry gotta hit you with it today!
15. Today I wanted to share with you that I started reading this awesome book called Blood, Sweat & Pixels. It's about the history of various indie and mainstream games and the struggle of the devs to get them done. I have to say it's very inspiring and it helps me on my own journey as a game dev. To be honest, I've been working on the same game for almost 2 years now and it can get a bit frustrating. Imagine having to hold your focus on one thing for so long (while juggling everyday life). However this book inspired me and helped me re-start my passion. Anyway, if you want to support the game, a simple wishlist would mean the world to me. Please check it out on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. 1. This here is a plushie duck that I'm hoping a lot of players will develop affection for. P.S. 2 HAVE A GREAT AND SUNNY WEEKEND, FRIENDOS!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: The American love of Ice Cream
item.description: Moar info: https://www.military.com/history/why-us-navy-operated-fleet-of-ice-cream-ships-during-world-war-ii.html Though Prohibition, which outlawed the manufacture and sale of alcohol in the United States, ended before the start of World War II, American industry needed to make products to fill the gaps booze left behind. More than one beer brewery converted its machinery to make ice cream, and soda fountains replaced saloons as gathering spots. Between 1916 and 1925, ice cream consumption grew by 55% among hungry Americans. At the beginning of World War II, the American ice cream factory was far removed from the labor-intensive, salt-and-ice operations of the 19th century. The consumer could buy ice cream at more outlets and in a greater variety of flavors and packages than ever before. Neither the end of Prohibition nor the Great Depression could stop the American ice-cream craze. So when the country entered World War II, it naturally became what we call today a "force multiplier." The U.S. Navy had (in)famously outlawed alcohol aboard ships in 1914, six years before Prohibition, but it still needed to fill the gap in morale boosting power a sailors daily drink left behind. As any noncommissioned officer who has overseen junior enlisted Americans will tell you: if you don't give them something to boost their morale, they can get into anything ... and you might not like what they find. video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OigDDVn3IaU
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: They're eating the pets ! They're eating the cats ! I'm not sure, look :
item.description:
2 :: Finally eating
Rooooky !
4 :: what is that foam, mate ??
Good job my cat !
6 :: A good catcher cat
Mesmerizing cat pull-up
8 :: Mother cat introduces her newborn kitten to owner's baby...
Psychologist: get a cat. Cats relieve stress. Me:
10 :: So brave
Last slap was personal though
12 :: Dual purpose Sphynx
Cat's grand entrance.
14 :: It's a Navy SEAL
It's so nice to lie there and watch the fish
16 :: That's amore
The best jump i've ever seen
18 :: When You Walk In On Your Cat Summoning Satan
why does your tail look like that ???
20 :: Yep, he's a call center agent.
Сat who knows his own worth
22 :: an evil laugh suits this cat perfectly
Almost all cats like to play with water
24 :: Funnnnny
A cat is indispensable in the home
26 :: I Bought a Tire to use as cat house
He works for the circus
28 :: monday morning vibes
No, don't lock me up
30 :: No pain no gain -- This cat can start the machine
she's like 'Yo! what's good'
32 :: He needs to be fed right away
Give me my shrimp
34 :: Feline, Best in Acting Awardee!
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: Viper – Doomsday (2008)
item.description:
Stuntwoman and actress Lee-Anne Liebenberg portrays Viper, a commander from a society of savages in a post-apocalyptic Scotland
Set 27 years after Scotland was quarantined due to a virus, the film follows a force led by Eden Sinclair (Rhona Mitra) that enters the forbidden zone to find a cure
This UK sci fi by Neil Marshall (Dog Soldiers, The Descent) unfortunately blends far more inspirations than it can handle, trading coherency and credibility for fast-cut spectacle
Its great cast (including Bob Hoskins, Malcolm McDowell, Alexander Siddig, Craig Conway, etc) has little to work with, yet there are still interesting elements
One of these is obviously the fell and styled up warrior who would have made the ancient Picts proud, or at least Sawney Bean
Despite a limited role was Viper elevated to the icon of the flick and is on many posters and covers
It is the best known acting role of Lee-Anne Liebenberg. As a stuntwoman she has credits as performer/double, coordinator, fight choreographer, and precision driver
Among the films she worked on are Avengers: Age of Ultron, Chappie, and District 9
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Happy International Women's Day
item.description:
Stephanie Louise Kwolek July 31, 1923 – June 18, 2014) was a Polish-American chemist who is known for inventing Kevlar. Her career at the DuPont company spanned more than 40 years. She discovered the first of a family of synthetic fibers of exceptional strength and stiffness: poly-paraphenylene terephthalamide.
Lizzie Magie Elizabeth J. Magie Phillips was an American game designer, writer, feminist, and Georgist. She invented The Landlord's Game, the precursor to Monopoly, to illustrate teachings of the progressive era economist Henry George.
Mary Elizabeth Anderson was an American real estate developer, rancher, viticulturist, and inventor of the windshield wiper blade. On November 10, 1903 Anderson was granted her first patent for an automatic car window cleaning device controlled from inside the car, called the windshield wiper.
Rosalind Franklin The discovery of the DNA double helix is often attributed to James Watson and Francis Click, who won the Nobel Prize for the discovery in 1962. However, they weren’t actually the ones who made the discovery. The first person to capture the photographic image using x-ray diffraction was British biophysicist Rosalind Franklin. Though her two competitors stole credit for the discovery long ago, Rosalind has more recently begun to receive credit for her contributions.
Judith Love Cohen (August 16, 1933 – July 25, 2016) was an American aerospace engineer. She was an electrical engineer on the Minuteman missile, the science ground station for the Hubble Space Telescope, the Tracking and Data Relay Satellite, and the Apollo Space Program.[2] In particular, her work on the Abort-Guidance System is credited with helping save Apollo 13. After her retirement from engineering, she founded a children's multimedia publishing company,[3] eventually publishing more than 20 titles before her death in 2016. She was the mother of computer scientist and engineer Neil Siegel and actor-musician Jack Black.
item.dc:creator: DropDrop
